title: Make Lexicographically Smallest Array by Swapping Elements
titleCn: 交换得到字典序最小的数组

testcases.input: nums = [1,5,3,9,8], limit = 2 Output: [1,3,5,8,9] Explanation: Apply the operation 2 times: - Swap nums[1] with nums[2]. The array becomes [1,3,5,9,8] - Swap nums[3] with nums[4]. The array becomes [1,3,5,8,9] We cannot obtain a lexicographically smaller array by applying any more operations. Note that it may be possible to get the same result by doing different operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: [1,3,5,8,9] Explanation: Apply the operation 2 times: - Swap nums[1] with nums[2]. The array becomes [1,3,5,9,8] - Swap nums[3] with nums[4]. The array becomes [1,3,5,8,9] We cannot obtain a lexicographically smaller array by applying any more operations. Note that it may be possible to get the same result by doing different operations.

testcases.input: nums = [1,7,6,18,2,1], limit = 3 Output: [1,6,7,18,1,2] Explanation: Apply the operation 3 times: - Swap nums[1] with nums[2]. The array becomes [1,6,7,18,2,1] - Swap nums[0] with nums[4]. The array becomes [2,6,7,18,1,1] - Swap nums[0] with nums[5]. The array becomes [1,6,7,18,1,2] We cannot obtain a lexicographically smaller array by applying any more operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: [1,6,7,18,1,2] Explanation: Apply the operation 3 times: - Swap nums[1] with nums[2]. The array becomes [1,6,7,18,2,1] - Swap nums[0] with nums[4]. The array becomes [2,6,7,18,1,1] - Swap nums[0] with nums[5]. The array becomes [1,6,7,18,1,2] We cannot obtain a lexicographically smaller array by applying any more operations.

testcases.input: nums = [1,7,28,19,10], limit = 3 Output: [1,7,28,19,10] Explanation: [1,7,28,19,10] is the lexicographically smallest array we can obtain because we cannot apply the operation on any two indices.
testcases.expectedOutput: [1,7,28,19,10] Explanation: [1,7,28,19,10] is the lexicographically smallest array we can obtain because we cannot apply the operation on any two indices.
